title: tangos.json
description: tangOS repo descriptor. One file per decomp repo that normalizes it into a single vocabulary read by every tangOS app (Console, Docs, Atlas). A repo's own tools are declared in `tools[]`; tangOS turns each entry into an MCP tool generically.

project.description: Human-facing identity + matching guidance. Superset of Chaos Viewer's project.config.json.

setup.description: How a contributor gets the repo runnable.

verifyCommand.description: Template command that verifies one function matches (placeholders like {name} {addrHex} {sizeHex}).

rules.description: Legal / contribution rules (ROM ownership, what may be committed).

submitting.description: How to open a PR: format, what may land in src/, where near-misses go. Surfaced to connected AIs at MCP startup and in next_batch. Point at an AGENTS.md in the repo for the full guide.

knownWalls.description: Proven-unreachable codegen shapes; surfaced to connected AIs via next_batch.

matchConventions.description: Optional matching conventions (attempt-tree logging, near-miss tip store, Ghidra scaffolds). Classic repos omit this; experimental forks set attemptTree so Console/next_batch surface MATCH_RESULT rules once per batch.

attemptTree.description: When true, MCP startup + next_batch include attempt-tree / MATCH_RESULT logging rules (shared once, not per target).

attemptsPath.description: Append-only attempt log path. Default: config/match_attempts.jsonl.

provenancePath.description: Final how-record path on bank. Default: config/match_provenance.jsonl.

ghidraDrafts.description: When true, agents may use local ghidra_out/ scaffolds as structure hints only (never bank as-is).

nearMissDb.description: Near-miss tip store path. Default: nearmiss/db.jsonl.

defaultProvenance.description: Prefill matchProvenance slugs for attempt-tree nodes (model / reasoning / harness).

runtime.description: How tangOS executes this repo's tools.

cwd.description: Working directory for tool commands, relative to the repo root. Default ".".

python.description: Python interpreter to use, e.g. "python", "python3", or a venv path. Default "python".

shell.description: Run commands through a shell (enables pipes/redirection). Default false (argv spawn).

envKeys.description: Names of environment variables some tools need (e.g. API keys). Values are NEVER stored here; tangOS reads them from the user's environment or prompts.

requirements.description: What the repo needs locally to actually run its tools. Surfaced to the user as a preflight checklist. tangOS does NOT ship any of these.

rom.description: Requires a user-supplied extracted ROM / game dump.

compiler.description: Name of a required external compiler the user must install, e.g. "mwccarm".

data.description: How tangOS Atlas obtains normalized progress/function data for this repo.

generate.description: Command that (re)generates the normalized data file. May use {out}.

dbPath.description: Repo-relative path the generate command writes to.

committedDbUrl.description: URL of a committed/published data file (e.g. a raw GitHub URL on a data branch). Lets tangOS pull the whole team's live progress/contributions. Highly recommended for collaborative projects.

claimsApi.description: Base URL of a claims/coordination service (e.g. https://belongto.us/api/claims) with a read-only GET /check?module=&start=&end= endpoint. Lets tangOS show who is working where.

categories.description: Optional display metadata for tool categories (order + label). Categories referenced by tools but absent here still render with defaults.

tools.description: The repo's tool registry. Each entry becomes one MCP tool in Console and one card in Docs.

id.description: Unique, MCP-tool-safe id (snake_case). Becomes the exposed tool name.

description.description: What the tool does - shown to the AI and in Docs.

readOnly.description: True if the tool only reads/reports and never mutates repo state (writes src/, commits, appends ledgers, hits mutating APIs). Drives the safety UI.

command.description: Command template. Supported placeholders: {python} (runtime.python), {flags} (rendered args), and {argName} for any declared arg. Example: "{python} tools/progress.py {flags}".

apply.description: For mutating tools that support a dry run: the flag that turns the dry run into a real mutation (e.g. "--apply"). tangOS defaults these tools to dry-run and requires explicit confirmation to pass this flag.

longRunning.description: Hint that this tool streams for a long time (orchestrators, batch runs). Default false.

needs.description: Preflight capabilities this tool requires: "rom", "compiler", "network", or "env:VAR_NAME".

args.description: Declared arguments. Rendered into {flags} in declaration order; also individually addressable as {argName}.

name.description: Arg name (snake_case). Also the MCP parameter name.

type.description: Value type. "boolean" renders the flag with no value when true; "enum" constrains to `choices`.

flag.description: CLI flag this arg renders as, e.g. "--module". Omit for a positional argument.

positional.description: Render as a bare positional value (no flag). Default false.

default.description: Default value when the arg is omitted.
